site.name: Concept Simulator
site.intro: Abstract ideas — optionality, convexity, luck — are hard to feel from prose. So here you don't read them. You play them. Each concept is a small, honestly-computed Monte-Carlo game: turn the parameters, watch the distribution move, and grow a gut feel that text never gives you.

taleb.name: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
taleb.note: Risk, antifragility, optionality. The spine of the whole family.

dredge.name: David Dredge
dredge.note: Convex tail hedging — turning the big loss into the source of compounding.

lecunff.name: Anne-Laure Le Cunff
lecunff.note: Tiny Experiments — loops over ladders, the PACT, reversible bets.

busch.name: Christian Busch
busch.note: The science of serendipity — manufacturing smart luck.

knight.name: Frank Knight
knight.note: Risk vs. uncertainty — the measurable and the genuinely unknown.

mandelbrot.name: Benoit Mandelbrot
mandelbrot.note: Power laws and the Lindy effect — life expectancy proportional to age.

meadows.name: Donella Meadows
meadows.note: Systems thinking — stocks, flows, feedback loops, and why a system's behavior comes from its structure, not its parts.

concepts.title: It's Just Math
concepts.blurb: Why avoiding the big loss compounds better than chasing the average gain — and why the number that describes the crowd is not the number you will live.

concepts.title: It's Just an Experiment
concepts.eyebrow: An interactive note on tiny experiments
concepts.blurb: Why a drifting world rewards loops over ladders, why a small repeatable promise is almost impossible to fail, and why many tiny reversible bets quietly beat one big plan.

concepts.title: It's Just Luck
concepts.eyebrow: An interactive note on manufacturing luck
concepts.blurb: Why good luck scales with how much of the world you touch, why an unexpected find is worthless until it meets a question you already carry, and why many small leads beat one big break.

concepts.title: It's Just Unknown
concepts.eyebrow: An interactive note on true uncertainty
concepts.blurb: Measurable risk vs. genuine uncertainty — how you act when the odds themselves are unknown, and why a known die is the easy case.

concepts.title: It's Just a Barbell
concepts.eyebrow: An interactive note on the barbell
concepts.blurb: Why 90% maximally safe plus 10% convex beats 100% "medium" — combining a hard floor with uncapped upside.

concepts.title: It's Just Old
concepts.eyebrow: An interactive note on the Lindy effect
concepts.blurb: Why people age forward and ideas age in reverse — for the non-perishable, every year survived buys more expected future, not less. The oldest bet is the safest bet.

concepts.title: It's Just a Loop
concepts.eyebrow: An interactive note on systems thinking
concepts.blurb: Why a system's behavior comes from its structure, not its parts — how a stock hides what its flows are doing, how a delay turns a steady loop into wild oscillation, and why growth into a limit so often overshoots and collapses.
